title: pittosporum viridiflorum subsp. angolense
intro: Pittosporum viridiflorum subsp. angolense, also known as the Angolan laurel or Ink berry, is a plant species within the Pittosporum viridiflorum complex. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Pittosporum viridiflorum subsp. angolense include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for stress and anxiety. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and ointments for topical application.
